summary: Remote code execution on module load via jsonkeeper.com fetch-and-eval
details: index.js auto-invokes a loader (initPlugin) that fetches a JSON document from https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/JTOGJ and passes its `cookiee` field to `new (Function.constructor)('require',...)`, then invokes the resulting function with `require` bound. This executes attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node.js module access in any process that require()s the package. The package advertises itself as an Express routing framework, but index.js contains no routing code — only a `normalizePath` stub and the remote-fetch-and-eval loader, with cover-story field names (`cookiee`, `bearrtoken: "logo"`). The fetch target is an anonymous paste-style host whose content the author can mutate at any time to deliver arbitrary payloads to installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
